text:
— Kyber NVL144, the flagship 2027 rack, delayed 12+ months to 2028. The PCB midplane can’t be manufactured at scale
— The backup architecture (NVL72x2) — cancelled
— The 4-die Rubin Ultra was already dead: packaging physically warps at that size. The 2-die survivor delivers roughly half the performance per package
— Optical scale-up (CPO) won’t arrive until Feynman

This is not one product slipping. It’s two physical walls — packaging AND interconnect — hitting in the same cycle. The “new rack every year” promise is what’s breaking.

WINNERS

— $GOOGL / $AVGO — the only proven alternative scale-up architecture on earth (optical switching, running in production for years). The 2027 window belongs to the TPU ecosystem
— $AMD — gets a window. A window is not a win. Execution at rack scale is unproven
— Pluggable optics: $COHR, $FN, $AAOI — CPO was the sword hanging over pluggables. It just got sheathed for another generation. The recent photonics selloff shot the wrong victim
— Copper: $APH, $CRDO — copper stays in the rack longer than anyone modeled
— Neoclouds: $NBIS, $CRWV — slower chip cadence means GPU fleets obsolete slower. The depreciation treadmill every bear cites just lost a year

LOSERS

— $NVDA — not revenue. They’ll sell every mature-gen rack they can make. What’s damaged is the moat premium: NVLink supremacy and the annual cadence ARE the multiple
— CPO pure plays: $POET, Sivers — the entire thesis is co-packaged optics adoption timing. That timing just moved right by a full generation. The biggest losers (Ayar Labs, Celestial AI) are private — you can’t even trade them
— $LITE — the trap. CPO laser story delayed, pluggable laser business extended. Net wash. Not the short people will think it is

THE $MU QUESTION

Do the math before you touch the sell button. Old Kyber rack: 144 packages x 16 HBM stacks = 2,304. New 2+2 config: 288 packages x 8 stacks = 2,304. Bit demand per rack: identical. What died is the “HBM per GPU doubles forever” narrative — a multiple story, not a bits story. And a flatter performance curve means buyers need MORE units for the same compute, in a market that’s supply-constrained until 2028.

Neutral on fundamentals. If it does trade down on the headline, then that gap will be called opportunity.

The nuance everyone will miss: NVIDIA can still hit rack-level compute targets with 2+2. Parity — bought with twice the silicon. The spec sheet survives. Cost per FLOP doesn’t.

The only question for 2027: can anyone exploit the gap before Feynman closes it? Exactly one company has a working alternative today.

Not financial advice.

The PCB midplane can’t be manufactured at scale — The backup architecture (NVL72x2) — cancelled — The 4-die Rubin Ultra was already dead: packaging physically warps at that size. The 2-die survivor delivers roughly half the performance per package — Optical scale-up (CPO) won’t arrive until Feynman This is not one product slipping. It’s two physical walls — packaging AND interconnect — hitting in the same cycle. The “new rack every year” promise is what’s breaking. WINNERS — $GOOGL / $AVGO — the only proven alternative scale-up architecture on earth (optical switching, running in production for years). The 2027 window belongs to the TPU ecosystem — $AMD — gets a window. A window is not a win. Execution at rack scale is unproven — Pluggable optics: $COHR, $FN, $AAOI — CPO was the sword hanging over pluggables. It just got sheathed for another generation. The recent photonics selloff shot the wrong victim — Copper: $APH, $CRDO — copper stays in the rack longer than anyone modeled — Neoclouds: $NBIS, $CRWV — slower chip cadence means GPU fleets obsolete slower. The depreciation treadmill every bear cites just lost a year LOSERS — $NVDA — not revenue. They’ll sell every mature-gen rack they can make. What’s damaged is the moat premium: NVLink supremacy and the annual cadence ARE the multiple — CPO pure plays: $POET, Sivers — the entire thesis is co-packaged optics adoption timing. That timing just moved right by a full generation. The biggest losers (Ayar Labs, Celestial AI) are private — you can’t even trade them — $LITE — the trap. CPO laser story delayed, pluggable laser business extended. Net wash. Not the short people will think it is THE $MU QUESTION Do the math before you touch the sell button. Old Kyber rack: 144 packages x 16 HBM stacks = 2,304. New 2+2 config: 288 packages x 8 stacks = 2,304. Bit demand per rack: identical. What died is the “HBM per GPU doubles forever” narrative — a multiple story, not a bits story. And a flatter performance curve means buyers need MORE units for the same compute, in a market that’s supply-constrained until 2028. Neutral on fundamentals. If it does trade down on the headline, then that gap will be called opportunity. The nuance everyone will miss: NVIDIA can still hit rack-level compute targets with 2+2. Parity — bought with twice the silicon. The spec sheet survives. Cost per FLOP doesn’t. The only question for 2027: can anyone exploit the gap before Feynman closes it? Exactly one company has a working alternative today. text:
Bullish $SIVE: AMD is building CPO for the MI500 (2027) through GlobalFoundries, bypassing NVIDIA’s TSMC packaging bottleneck entirely. Since June, Sivers’ laser arrays are integrated into GF’s silicon photonics reference designs and SCALE CPO platform. If AMD’s optical scale-up bet materializes at GF, Sivers lasers are in the supply chain. NVIDIA stumbling widens exactly the window AMD and its photonics partners need.

Bullish $AAOI: every year CPO slips at NVIDIA is another year pluggable transceivers remain the workhorse of AI datacenters. Delays don’t destroy optical demand, they redirect capital back to pluggables and NPO. That’s AAOI’s core business. The market dumped it 17% on the delay headline without asking who actually benefits from it.

One company wins if CPO accelerates outside NVIDIA. The other wins because it’s delayed inside NVIDIA. Both win from the same news.

Delays don’t kill demand. They redistribute it.

text:
🔭 Hawking Radiation — No Longer Just Theory?

Scientists have experimentally observed the *backreaction* of stimulated Hawking radiation using an optical analogue — a fiber-optic system that mimics the physics of a black hole's event horizon. This is a landmark step: Hawking radiation was predicted in 1974, but direct observation near real black holes remains impossible. Optical analogues let physicists probe quantum effects at the event horizon in a lab setting. The backreaction — how emitted radiation affects the "horizon" itself — is key to understanding black hole evaporation and the famous information paradox. This experiment brings us closer to resolving one of physics' deepest mysteries. 🌌

Companies in this space: Coherent Corp. ($COHR, NYSE), II-VI / MKS Instruments ($MKSI, NASDAQ), IPG Photonics ($IPGP, NASDAQ)

text: @aleabitoreddit Should we sell all the photonics and follow you full port to the robots ???

🤖 10 หุ้นที่น่าจับตาในธีม Robotics ปี 2026

$TER – Teradyne ผู้นำด้านเครื่องทดสอบชิป
เป็นเจ้าของ Universal Robots ผู้พัฒนาหุ่นยนต์ร่วมงาน (Cobots) สำหรับภาคอุตสาหกรรม

$NOVT – Novanta พัฒนาเทคโนโลยีโฟโตนิกส์ (Photonics) และระบบควบคุมการเคลื่อนไหวความแม่นยำสูง
ใช้ในหุ่นยนต์ผ่าตัดและหุ่นยนต์อุตสาหกรรม

$OUST – Ouster พัฒนาเซ็นเซอร์ LiDAR ที่ทำหน้าที่เป็น "ดวงตา" ของหุ่นยนต์
ช่วยให้หุ่นยนต์มองเห็น ทำแผนที่ และรับรู้สภาพแวดล้อม

$HIMX – Himax Technologiesผลิตชิปประมวลผลภาพและชิปแสดงผล
ช่วยให้หุ่นยนต์ประมวลผลภาพ ความลึก และข้อมูลจากเซ็นเซอร์ต่างๆ ได้ที่ตัวอุปกรณ์

$AMBQ – Ambiq Microพัฒนาชิป AI แบบประหยัดพลังงาน
เหมาะสำหรับหุ่นยนต์และอุปกรณ์ Edge AI ที่ต้องการประมวลผลอัจฉริยะโดยใช้พลังงานต่ำ

$ON – ON Semiconductor กำลังสร้างแพลตฟอร์ม Edge AI สำหรับหุ่นยนต์ หลังเข้าซื้อ $SYNA – Synaptics
รวมเทคโนโลยีด้านพลังงาน เซ็นเซอร์ การเชื่อมต่อ และ Edge AI ไว้ในระบบเดียว

$SYM – Symbotic ผู้เชี่ยวชาญด้านระบบอัตโนมัติในคลังสินค้า
ใช้หุ่นยนต์และซอฟต์แวร์เพื่อเพิ่มประสิทธิภาพการจัดเก็บและขนส่งสินค้า

$VPG – Vishay Precision Group พัฒนาเซ็นเซอร์วัดแรงและระบบวัดความแม่นยำสูง
ช่วยให้หุ่นยนต์รับรู้แรงกด น้ำหนัก และการเคลื่อนไหวในโลกจริงได้แม่นยำขึ้น

$TSLA – Tesla เดิมพันกับหุ่นยนต์ Humanoid ผ่าน Optimus
ผสานฮาร์ดแวร์ AI ระบบขับเคลื่อนอัตโนมัติ และศักยภาพการผลิตขนาดใหญ่ของบริษัท

$CCXI – Churchill Capital Corp XI
เปิดโอกาสให้นักลงทุนเข้าถึง Agility Robotics
บริษัทผู้พัฒนาหุ่นยนต์ Humanoid Digit ซึ่งเป็นหนึ่งในผู้เล่นที่โดดเด่นด้านหุ่นยนต์สำหรับคลังสินค้า

text:
$STM, STMicroelectronics. STM is one of Europe's largest chipmakers, and it builds its own chips end-to-end, from design all the way through its own factories. Its parts are the unglamorous but essential pieces inside almost everything — the sensors, power chips, and tiny "brains" (microcontrollers) inside cars, phones, factory machines, satellites, robots, and now AI data centers.

What makes STM unique is the sheer breadth: it holds a #1 or #2 position in an unusual number of separate chip categories at once (SiC power, MEMS sensors, STM32 microcontrollers), which lets a customer like NVIDIA source a whole system — sensors, brain, power, and communications — from one vendor. The reason it's suddenly interesting is that a company written off for two years as a "sleepy automotive chipmaker" just found a real AI growth engine: on June 2 it doubled its 2026 data-center revenue target to ~$1B (with a path to ~$2B in 2027), anchored by a multi-year AWS silicon-photonics deal, while sitting on ~$2.8B net cash. The investment case is a cyclical turnaround (Q1 revenue +23% YoY, up ~90%+ off February lows) layered on top of a genuine new secular driver, with the biggest names in tech — AWS, NVIDIA — as partners.

Thesis: STM spent two years being ignored as a boring car-chip company, but it just quietly turned into an AI company — its parts now go inside Amazon and NVIDIA's data centers, and that business is set to double two years in a row. The stock has already jumped a lot, so the easy money is gone, but the real question is whether its profit margins can climb back to normal as sales recover — if they do, the stock is still cheap for what it's about to earn. I'm watching the July 23 earnings to see if those margins are finally turning up, because that's the part the market hasn't fully believed yet.

text:
spent the morning reading the $LAZR filing. semi analysis teamed up with tema to launch a pure-play optics etf and honestly it makes sense. the memory bottleneck got DISK. now the data movement bottleneck gets its own wrapper.

top holding is lumentum at 14.6%. they make the lasers and transceivers that move data between racks at ai scale. second biggest position is literally cash at 11.4% which tells you this thing just launched and they're still deploying.

the interesting name here is aixtron at 10.5%. german compound semi equipment. mocvd deposition tools that build the substrates optical chips run on. axti at 9.7% is the substrate play itself. applied opto at 5.8% is the highest beta name in the basket.

goldman has the photonics market doing 9x between now and 2028. copper hits a wall. silicon photonics stops being a niche and becomes the thing everything rides on. same pattern we saw with memory when DISK launched.

the optics layer is where the next capacity crunch forms. watch the holdings more than the etf itself. the basket tells you where the spend is heading before the earnings do.

"text": "spent the morning reading the $LAZR filing. semi analysis teamed up with tema to launch a pure-play optics etf and honestly it makes sense. the memory bottleneck got DISK. now the data movement bottleneck gets its own wrapper. top holding is lumentum at 14.6%. they make the lasers and transceivers that move data between racks at ai scale. second biggest position is literally cash at 11.4% which tells you this thing just launched and they're still deploying. the interesting name here is aixtron at 10.5%. german compound semi equipment. mocvd deposition tools that build the substrates optical chips run on. axti at 9.7% is the substrate play itself. applied opto at 5.8% is the highest beta name in the basket. goldman has the photonics market doing 9x between now and 2028. copper hits a wall. silicon photonics stops being a niche and becomes the thing everything rides on. same pattern we saw with memory when DISK launched. the optics layer is where the next capacity crunch forms. watch the holdings more than the etf itself. the basket tells you where the spend is heading before the earnings do."}}}, "rest_id": "2073166857516023812", "source": "Questflow App", text:
Fiber & Infrastructure Highlights – July 3, 2026

GoNetspeed announced a $43M expansion in Rahway, Clark, Linden, and Woodbridge, New Jersey for over 34,000 homes and businesses. Persistent fiber shortages continue with ribbon lead times at 60+ weeks (AI/data center priority) and loose tube into Q3 2026+; BABA-compliant glass remains limited. Corning’s hyperscaler deals (up to $6B with Meta for NC expansion including new Hickory facility; multi-billion with Amazon) and manufacturing ramps (Nvidia-funded plants) address demand. FiberLight’s $350M West Texas expansion (1,400 route miles, high-count dark fiber for AI) and total ~$500M regional investment continue. Ripple Fiber has multiple expansions (WA $250M for 200k+ locations, IL, AZ $80M, CA) with ongoing construction.

Vocus Sydney-Melbourne ducted long-haul (~A$500M, ready 2029), Lightpath NY metro (+265 miles for 2,400+ towers), Heartland Fiber Project, Big Fiber financing, and various BEAD/FTTH builds add to activity. Fiber vendors (Corning, Fujikura tripling capacity, STL, CommScope) scale high-count, multi-core, and photonics solutions amid AI’s 36x fiber density needs. FBA whitepaper on fiber-AI convergence and power/grid constraints highlight sustained physical layer momentum into 2026.

#FiberForge #FiberOptics #OSPISP

text:
Im not personally in photonics, i just share this info because it may be of use to someone. 

That said, i believe only a handful of names in this market are actually going to be successful LT (marvell, lumentum, coherent, etc). CPO is still early...you wont see meaningful revenue until 2027-2028. A lot of people are getting ahead and investing in overvalued photonics stocks, hence the sharp pullback its see the past few weeks. At some point the valuation will matter, and its mattering now.
@pequityresearch Excellent breakdown of the Silicon Photonics supply chain. Very informative and well-organized. Great work highlighting the key players across the ecosystem.

text:
If you have better companies or sectors to buy, then selling half can definitely make sense.

I already have exposure to most sectors where I see major upside: defence, photonics, memory, and space. So overall, I'm quite happy with the distribution.

At most, I might rotate some direct exposure into robotics, but I'm still not fully convinced, especially on humanoids. Maybe I'm biased because of EU regulations...

text:
Sivers Semiconductors (https://t.co/8VMNAF5r3U / SIVEF) has strong long-term growth potential in high-margin photonics and wireless tech sectors, but faces near-term dilution pressure, execution risks, and valuation challenges following this news.

What the News Means
•Bootstrap Europe is converting its full $12M convertible loan (part of a $17M refinancing from Feb 2026) into 22,847,044 new ordinary shares at SEK 4.77 per share.

•This eliminates that debt (positive for the balance sheet and interest costs) and simplifies the capital structure.

•Dilution impact: With ~296 million shares outstanding pre-conversion, this adds ~7-8% more shares. It’s already anticipated (disclosed in Feb 2026, with conversion price at a premium back then), but it increases the float and can create selling pressure if Bootstrap sells shares.

•The stock jumped ~15% today (aligning with your “16+ plus”), likely because the conversion removes debt overhang and signals confidence from the lender (who is now a shareholder).

Short-Term Outlook (Next 3-12 Months)
•Positive drivers: The conversion cleans up the balance sheet. The company has a massive ~$800M opportunity pipeline (up 77% YTD as of Q1), with catalysts like product ramps in 2027 (LiDAR, SATCOM, fixed wireless, defense, and potential AI/data center optics). Q2 results are due around early August.15
•Headwinds: Significant dilution (this event + recent/ongoing directed share issues for funding). The stock is volatile and has pulled back from highs near SEK 110. High valuation (very elevated P/S multiples relative to current revenue) makes it sensitive to any delays or misses. Recent sector selloffs in photonics (e.g., CPO concerns) have added pressure.11
•Expect continued volatility. Bootstrap may sell gradually (or hold), but the increased share count could cap upside until positive news dominates.
Medium-to-Long-Term Future (2027+)
•Bull case (optimistic): Strong execution on the pipeline could drive ~30%+ annual revenue growth, with breakeven/profitability targeted for 2027. Key markets (AI optics, LiDAR production ramps, SATCOM/defense) have high potential. Analysts forecast sharp earnings improvement. A Nasdaq dual-listing could boost visibility and liquidity.1965
•Base case: Revenue scales with product ramps, but competition and execution risks (delays in customer adoption) remain. Dilution from this and other raises will mute per-share gains.
•Risks: Still pre-profit with cash burn, dependency on converting the pipeline into actual revenue, sector hype cycles, and further equity raises. High valuation leaves little room for error. $Sivef

Overall: This news is neutral-to-slightly positive fundamentally (debt reduction) but adds immediate dilution. The company’s future hinges on 2027+ ramps materializing in photonics/wireless. It’s a high-risk, high-reward growth story suited for investors who believe in the long-term AI/semiconductor tailwinds and can tolerate volatility. Not financial advice—do your own research, consider the full financials, and monitor upcoming reports. $Sive

RILEY SAYS AMAZON'S RNG & OPENAI'S MRC PROTOCOLS POSE MAJOR STRUCTURAL RISK FOR OPTICAL INFRASTRUCTURE / "PHOTONICS" STOCKS, NAMELY APPLIED OPTOELECTRONICS $AAOI Full comments: "Following the OFC conference in March 2025, the AI data center boom triggered an unprecedented optical supercycle. As demonstrated by the parabolic re-rating of optical infrastructure stocks such as Lumentum ($LITE – Buy, $1,142 PT ) and Coherent ($COHR – Neutral, $309 PT ), the prevailing investment thesis assumed an aggressive, compounding multiplier: scaling AI clusters meant building deeper, multi-tiered networks (3-tier or 4-tier Clos topologies), exponentially driving up transceiver demand per unit of compute. This thesis, however, is hitting a structural wall. Just a year after the 2025 explosion, the simultaneous market introduction of Amazon’s ($AMZN, NR) Resilient Network Graphs (RNG) and OpenAI’s Multipath Reliable Connection (MRC) protocol marks a major pivot toward network flattening across both training and inference workloads. Microsoft ($MSFT, NR) and Oracle ($ORCL, NR) are actively integrating the open-source MRC protocol into their frontier AI training supercomputing clusters—such as ORCL’s massive GB200 Abilene site—to execute sender-controlled packet spraying and bypass physical network bottlenecks. Concurrently, AMZN is deploying its quasi-random RNG architecture across its general-purpose cloud fabric. While RNG is isolated from back-end training clusters, it serves as the primary backbone for scale-out generative AI inference, data ingestion, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). Crucially, as mature AI models transition from training to mass inference, hyperscaler capex is shifting heavily in favor of these front-end, scale-out environments. Because RNG replaces traditional, active multi-tiered aggregation switches with passive optical ShuffleBoxes, it reduces active networking equipment by over 60%. Consequently, as AI workloads migrate to inference, this architectural deflation loops directly into the broader optical market, stripping out switch-to-switch optical links and dismantling the legacy transceiver-to-compute multiplier. For the transceiver market, this dual-front flattening represents a severe structural headwind that will sharply decelerate the sector's long-term TAM expansion, since these technologies reduce structural transceiver counts by 40% to 50%. We believe Applied Optoelectronics ($AAOI – Neutral, $129 PT) will be especially vulnerable to this development. AMZN and ORCL—the primary network provider to OpenAI—are expected to be the anchor customers for the 800G/1.6T transceivers forecast to drive AAOI's quarterly revenue to ~$1B by 2H27. As ORCL deflates training-cluster transceiver ratios via MRC, and AMZN strips active transceiver requirements from inference rollouts via RNG, AAOI's core revenue growth engines face massive structural risk.""}}}, "rest_id": "2074071756109656328", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "423511", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2074282003319452014", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "40", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$sive is real!

우리에게 생소할 수 있지만, 글로벌 데이터센터, 클라우드, 인프라 업계에서는 매우 높은 신뢰도와 영향력을 가진 대표적인 전문 B2B 미디어인 ‘Data Center Frontier'라는 곳에서,

AI 클러스터가 거대해짐에 따라 기존 구리선 기반 연결의 한계를 극복하기 위해 광학 기술 및 실리콘 포토닉스를 데이터센터 인프라에 도입해야 한다는 내용을 다룸. 

그리고 이 기사는 6시간 전에 올라온 기사.

이 기사는 데이터 센터 프론티어의 시니어 에디터인 David Chernicoff가 작성했으며,

중요한 건 $sive 관련 내용이 ‘굵직하게’ 포함됨. 

총 5가지 내용 중에 심지어 한 부분을 차지함. 

(원문 발췌)
“Those developments are now being reinforced by a broader series of moves across the optical ecosystem:
- Corning’s major AI infrastructure partnerships with NVIDIA and Amazon,
- GlobalFoundries’ push into co-packaged optics,
- Sivers’ laser-array collaboration with GlobalFoundries, $sive
- Wiwynn’s co-packaged optics demonstration at Computex,
- Credo’s acquisition of DustPhotonics, and emerging near-packaged optical interconnect designs from LightSpeed Photonics.”

기사의 $sive 관련 세부내용으로는,

1. $sive와 글로벌 파운드리스 $gfs 와의 레이저 어레이 협력 명시

- Sivers의 레이저 어레이(Laser arrays)는 글로벌파운드리의 실리콘 포토닉스 플랫폼을 기반으로 제작된 레퍼런스 디자인(참조 설계)에 통합되며, GF의 SCALE 광학 엔진 솔루션으로 제공될 예정. 

2. Wiwynn의 CPO 안의 Ayar labs 명시

- 알다시피 Ayar labs의 SuperNova에 $sive 가 들어감

이 두 내용을 이런 미디어의 기사에서 명시했다는 건 $sive 가 진짜라는 걸 간접적으로 확인해주는 것이지 않을까?

이 데이터 센터 프론티어라는 미디어는, 

독자층이 주로 구글, 마이크로소프트, 메타, AWS 같은 하이퍼스케일러(Hyperscaler)의 인프라 엔지니어링 임원, 데이터센터 아키텍트, 설계·건설 책임자, 그리고 데이터센터 리츠(REITs) 투자가들이라고 하고, 

독자의 약 50% 이상이 C-Level(경영진)이나 VP, 디렉터급으로 구성되어 있어, 실질적인 인프라 투자와 기술 도입에 중요한 이정표가 된다고 함. 

기사 원문 링크는 댓글 참고

$sive 살려

text:
Be careful on $AAOI.

There's a crazy amount of resentment from institutions such as B. Riley who MISSED the stock and were bearish as low as $15.

Now making a massive intellectual leap on a nonsensical optical short thesis and literally name dropping $AAOI out of bitterness? Spite? For the virality?

You really can't make up the misinformation and FUD in photonics right now it's just unbelievable. Earnings should put everyone in their place very soon. Nothing has changed in the fact that there isn't enough photonics tech in the world today and won't be for years.

Everyone in my paid Substack + Discord knows I've been buying $AAOI all the way down from $160 in my trading portfolio, most recently loading at $114. I also increased $AAOI in my "Photonics Is Next" portfolio today.

No asymmetry without FUD - NAWF.

RILEY SAYS AMAZON'S RNG & OPENAI'S MRC PROTOCOLS POSE MAJOR STRUCTURAL RISK FOR OPTICAL INFRASTRUCTURE / "PHOTONICS" STOCKS, NAMELY APPLIED OPTOELECTRONICS $AAOI Full comments: "Following the OFC conference in March 2025, the AI data center boom triggered an unprecedented optical supercycle. As demonstrated by the parabolic re-rating of optical infrastructure stocks such as Lumentum ($LITE – Buy, $1,142 PT ) and Coherent ($COHR – Neutral, $309 PT ), the prevailing investment thesis assumed an aggressive, compounding multiplier: scaling AI clusters meant building deeper, multi-tiered networks (3-tier or 4-tier Clos topologies), exponentially driving up transceiver demand per unit of compute. This thesis, however, is hitting a structural wall. Just a year after the 2025 explosion, the simultaneous market introduction of Amazon’s ($AMZN, NR) Resilient Network Graphs (RNG) and OpenAI’s Multipath Reliable Connection (MRC) protocol marks a major pivot toward network flattening across both training and inference workloads. Microsoft ($MSFT, NR) and Oracle ($ORCL, NR) are actively integrating the open-source MRC protocol into their frontier AI training supercomputing clusters—such as ORCL’s massive GB200 Abilene site—to execute sender-controlled packet spraying and bypass physical network bottlenecks. Concurrently, AMZN is deploying its quasi-random RNG architecture across its general-purpose cloud fabric. While RNG is isolated from back-end training clusters, it serves as the primary backbone for scale-out generative AI inference, data ingestion, and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). Crucially, as mature AI models transition from training to mass inference, hyperscaler capex is shifting heavily in favor of these front-end, scale-out environments. Because RNG replaces traditional, active multi-tiered aggregation switches with passive optical ShuffleBoxes, it reduces active networking equipment by over 60%. Consequently, as AI workloads migrate to inference, this architectural deflation loops directly into the broader optical market, stripping out switch-to-switch optical links and dismantling the legacy transceiver-to-compute multiplier. For the transceiver market, this dual-front flattening represents a severe structural headwind that will sharply decelerate the sector's long-term TAM expansion, since these technologies reduce structural transceiver counts by 40% to 50%. We believe Applied Optoelectronics ($AAOI – Neutral, $129 PT) will be especially vulnerable to this development. AMZN and ORCL—the primary network provider to OpenAI—are expected to be the anchor customers for the 800G/1.6T transceivers forecast to drive AAOI's quarterly revenue to ~$1B by 2H27. As ORCL deflates training-cluster transceiver ratios via MRC, and AMZN strips active transceiver requirements from inference rollouts via RNG, AAOI's core revenue growth engines face massive structural risk.""}}}, "rest_id": "2074071756109656328", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "423511", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2074273679697961422", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "132806", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
An ETF shop just filed a pure-play photonics ETF. That tells you as much about the top as it does about the opportunity.

$LYTE is Roundhill's new Photonics and Optics ETF. It is filed with the SEC but not trading yet. When it launches it will hold the optics pure-plays powering AI data centers: names like Lumentum, Coherent, POET, AAOI, IPG, and Ciena.

The pitch writes itself. If you believe in the photonics buildout but you cannot say whether $LITE, $COHR, or $POET wins, you own the group, not the single-name risk. That is a good argument for the basket.

But two things are worth saying out loud.

First, both the ETF and the single names are falling knives right now. This is a theme-wide de-rating. LITE knifed lower, AXTI is down 54% off its high, the whole optics complex is bleeding together. An ETF spreads single-name risk. It does nothing when the entire group falls. And LYTE is not even technically diversified. Its own filing calls it non-diversified, a concentrated basket of a handful of optics names.

Second, thematic ETFs tend to launch near tops. Roundhill built this because its DRAM ETF was a smash, hitting $1B in 10 days. Then memory crashed. An issuer racing to package a hot theme right as it corrects is a yellow flag, not a green light.

The move is not ETF or stock. It is neither, yet. Wait for the optics group to base, then use both: the ETF as the diversified core once it trades, the single name as the tactical piece you can sell puts on.

Not financial advice.

text:
He called $SIVE | $MU | $SNDK | $NBIS | $AXTI

These aren't random picks.

They're all solving critical bottlenecks in the AI trade:

Memory
Photonics
Neoclouds

The pieces of the supply chain that have to exist for the entire stack to work.

That's the difference between someone actually doing homework and someone just drawing lines on a chart.

For context, the fund achieved this return holding a diverse basket of stocks, an incredible outcome for retail investors passively following. I'm extremely proud of our work on this product. text:
We are seeking a highly motivated and enthusiastic Electronics Engineer to join our team and contribute to our mission in accelerating the growth and adoption of integrated photonics technologies.

▫️Job Title: Electronics Engineer
▫️Job Type: Full-time
▫️Salary: £50k – £70k pa., depending on experience
▫️Location: Cambridge, East of England, England
▫️Start Date: August 2026 or ASAP

📍Visa Sponsorship is available to those already in the UK 🇬🇧 

Qualifications 
✅️ Degree in Electronic Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Physics, or a related discipline.
✅️ Strong software development skills for embedded programming in C.
✅️ Python/MATLAB experience for measurement automation, data analysis, test and verification.
✅️ Commitment to facilitate effective cross-team knowledge transfer through clear documentation.

Apply here: https://t.co/Fq5Hqn0QQr

Please note: This Job is open to only those currently in the UK. Those outside the UK should not apply 

Please share this with others in the UK who may be Interested

text:
🟩🟩🟩🟩🟩🟩🟩🟩🟩🟩🟩
 30-Second Stock Market Review
 🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥🟥  

1️⃣ $META up 3% on a positive SemiAnalysis report stating that capex for 2027 is likely to remain intact, as the AI thesis remains solid. 
2️⃣ $BE up 8% amid ongoing volatility after the company recently announced a $25B framework from Brookfield to expand AI infrastructure. 
3️⃣ $AMD up 6% after Goldman Sachs raised its price target to $640. 
4️⃣ $IREN up 13% as AI compute and crypto stocks rallied. 
5️⃣ $TSLA up 6% amid recent production and delivery figures, along with momentum in robotics and physical AI stocks. 
6️⃣ $TSM up 4% on reports that the company is expected to raise its full-year revenue outlook to nearly 40% growth, up from 30%.
7️⃣ $CRDO up 10% as stocks in the optics and photonics sectors had relief rallies on Monday. 
8️⃣ $RKLB down 7% as space-themed stocks traded broadly lower. 
9️⃣ $DELL up 4% on positive comments from President Trump, urging consumers to buy the company’s products. 
🔟 $WULF up 4% on news that the company executed a 20-year lease deal with Anthropic at its data campus site in Kentucky. Also, the company signed an agreement to sell a 50.1% stake in the Abernathy joint venture to an investor group.

Market News

🆕 Bitcoin rose to nearly $64K on Monday after Trump made positive comments on crypto. 
🆕 Semiconductor stocks rallied after a nearly two-week slump. 

Upcoming Events 7/7/26

➡️ ADP Employment Change Weekly
➡️ 3-Year Note Auction
➡️ Earnings: $PENG, $EPAC, $KRUS, $SAR

*Information is from sources believed to be reliable. CenterPoint Securities cannot guarantee the accuracy or completeness of such information. This material should not be viewed as a recommendation to buy or sell any securities or to adopt any investment strategy.

While AAOI’s primary business is building traditional pluggable optical transceivers, they also develop foundational high-power lasers and external laser sources for CPO architectures. So they’re bridging the gap today while also prepping for what comes next. CPO is a 2028/29 story at best. But the point is having a fundamental basis to justify a stock price today. Whether on a visible discounted cash flow basis or just material revenue/backlog growth TODAY. AAOI has explosive revenue growth actually materializing, on top of a surging **backlog**, whereas Sivers is baiting retail with a merely subjective **opportunity pipeline**, with zero photonics revenue today, let alone even a backlog to show for anything. Just non-exclusive partnerships on reference designs that they hope will be used by 2028. And pricing power will be fully at the discretion of WIN Semi, who they outsource designs to, further eroding potential margins should anything even manifest. I mean hey, this thing could surely pump again, but the risks are IMMENSE and it will only run on hype, which is intrinsically fizzling out (especially with all this dilution and some serious macro risks on the horizon/liquidity tightening). I suspect some funky business going on with their management, and I’m merely trying to make people aware not to blindly follow anonymous pump pages

text:
Paradis Labs ยังมั่นใจ $AAOI และ $AEHR แม้หุ้น AI Small Cap ปรับฐานแรง

Paradis Labs ระบุว่ายังคงถือหุ้น AAOI และ AEHR พร้อมแสดงความเชื่อมั่นต่อแนวโน้มระยะยาวของทั้งสองบริษัท แม้ว่าหุ้นกลุ่ม AI Infrastructure ขนาดเล็กจะถูกแรงขายกดดันในช่วงที่ผ่านมา

สำหรับ AAOI มองว่า

ตั้งเป้ารายได้จาก Optical Transceiver แตะประมาณ 471 ล้านดอลลาร์ต่อเดือนภายในกลางปี 2027
บริษัทกำลังขยายกำลังการผลิตรองรับผลิตภัณฑ์ 800G และ 1.6T
แม้เพิ่มกำลังผลิตแล้ว คาดว่าอุปสงค์จะยังสูงกว่ากำลังการผลิตจนถึงประมาณไตรมาส 3 ปี 2027

ส่วน AEHR

เพิ่งได้รับคำสั่งซื้อเพิ่มเติมสำหรับระบบ Wafer Level Burn-In (WLBI) จากลูกค้า Silicon Photonics
มีโอกาสได้รับคำสั่งซื้อเพิ่มภายในปีนี้ หลังผ่านการรับรองเป็นซัพพลายเออร์แล้ว
ก่อนหน้านี้ยังได้รับคำสั่งซื้อระบบ Package Level Burn-In (PLBI) จากลูกค้า Hyperscaler รายใหญ่

Paradis Labs ระบุว่า หุ้นทั้งสองตัวมีความผันผวนสูง (High Beta) จึงถูกแรงขายในรอบ AI Selloff มากกว่าหุ้นขนาดใหญ่ แต่ยังเชื่อมั่นในแนวโน้มระยะยาวและยังคงถือการลงทุนต่อ

With $AAOI:
> Target ~$471M monthly transceiver revenue mid-2027.
> Capacity ramp is happening now for 800G/1.6T.
> Even with capacity expansion, demand is expected to exceed production capacity until ~Q3

text:
Be warned!! This is a little long but VERY informative on who Kopin should be prospecting and timeline of execution.

I KNOW we are early on $KOPN with its new MicroLED Neural I/o chip design. And its DEMO to be presented end of 2026. So trying to take advantage while their is time, #KOPIN should be looking into hyperscalers first. 

Hyperscalers & Cloud AI Builders (Top Priority)

NVIDIA (GPU clusters, NVLink complements), Google (TPUs), Amazon (Trainium/Inferentia), Microsoft (Azure Maia/custom silicon), Meta, Oracle, xAI, and others building massive AI training/inference clusters.

WHY? 
They face acute power and bandwidth walls in dense GPU racks. Power/cooling is a top TCO driver; Neural I/o promises major efficiency gains for intra-rack or short inter-rack GPU-to-GPU and GPU-to-memory traffic.
AI Chip/Accelerator Designers
NVIDIA, AMD, Broadcom (custom ASICs), and startups (Cerebras, Groq, SambaNova, etc.). Also advanced packaging players (TSMC, Intel, Samsung) for chiplet or co-packaged optics (CPO) integration.

WHY? 
Direct integration into next-gen accelerators or 2.5D/3D packages. Supports high-parallelism needs without heavy SerDes bottlenecks.

Server/System OEMs & Integrators Supermicro, Dell, HPE, Lenovo.

WHY? 
They build the racks/servers sold to hyperscalers. Better interconnects enable higher-density, lower-power systems that differentiate their offerings. Networking & Photonics Ecosystem Broadcom (AVGO—networking ASICs/optics leader), Arista, Cisco, plus photonics firms (Lumentum/LITE, Coherent). Potential partners or complementors rather than pure competitors for specific short-reach use cases.

Defense, Government & Sovereign AI Infrastructure

DoD/intelligence community data centers, contractors, and sovereign AI projects (Europe, Middle East, Asia) seeking secure/domestic supply chains.

WHY?
Kopin’s US-based full MicroLED production capability + long defense heritage (displays, soldier systems) provides a strong moat for secure AI hardware. Dual-use potential with existing defense MicroLED work.

Advantages of $KOPN vs. Current Data Center Interconnects

Neural I/o sits in a “sweet spot” between copper’s limitations and traditional laser optics’ drawbacks for short-to-medium reach, high-bandwidth AI intra-cluster links. It draws from “wide-and-slow” parallel channel concepts (hundreds/thousands of lower-speed MicroLED channels instead of a few ultra-high-speed lanes), similar to Microsoft Research’s MOSAIC project (which demonstrated ~56-68% lower power, up to 50m reach, and much higher reliability using MicroLEDs + imaging fiber).Neural I/o wins on bandwidth scalability (parallelism enables 1.6 Tbps demo target soon, 3.6 Tbps roadmap), dramatically lower power per bit, less heat (easier cooling, higher rack density), and better reach for board/rack scales. It “breaks the copper wall” while keeping costs and power closer to copper-like economics for these reaches.Vs. Traditional Optical (InP/VCSEL lasers, silicon photonics transceivers, pluggable optics or CPO):
Lasers excel at longer reach but are power-hungry, expensive, thermally sensitive, complex (heavy DSP, CDR, FEC, equalization, SerDes), and overkill (plus higher failure rates) for many short intra-rack or chip-to-chip links in AI clusters. They add cost, power, and complexity where not fully needed.

Neural I/o wins by using MicroLEDs as direct emitters (cheaper, lower power, more temperature-robust, simpler drive electronics possible with parallel low-speed channels). It eliminates or reduces need for expensive laser modules and complex digital backends for targeted use cases. Potential for smaller form factor, higher reliability/redundancy (pixel-level), and better power efficiency (comparable to MOSAIC’s ~3-5W vs. 10-12W per link range in research). Bidirectional capability from NeuralDisplay architecture is a plus. It avoids “expensive laser-based systems entirely” for these applications while delivering needed bandwidth.

LOOONG $KOPN

Supports high-parallelism needs without heavy SerDes bottlenecks. Server/System OEMs & Integrators Supermicro, Dell, HPE, Lenovo. WHY? They build the racks/servers sold to hyperscalers. Better interconnects enable higher-density, lower-power systems that differentiate their offerings. Networking & Photonics Ecosystem Broadcom (AVGO—networking ASICs/optics leader), Arista, Cisco, plus photonics firms (Lumentum/LITE, Coherent). Potential partners or complementors rather than pure competitors for specific short-reach use cases. Defense, Government & Sovereign AI Infrastructure DoD/intelligence community data centers, contractors, and sovereign AI projects (Europe, Middle East, Asia) seeking secure/domestic supply chains. WHY? Kopin’s US-based full MicroLED production capability + long defense heritage (displays, soldier systems) provides a strong moat for secure AI hardware. Dual-use potential with existing defense MicroLED work. Advantages of $KOPN vs. Current Data Center Interconnects Neural I/o sits in a “sweet spot” between copper’s limitations and traditional laser optics’ drawbacks for short-to-medium reach, high-bandwidth AI intra-cluster links. It draws from “wide-and-slow” parallel channel concepts (hundreds/thousands of lower-speed MicroLED channels instead of a few ultra-high-speed lanes), similar to Microsoft Research’s MOSAIC project (which demonstrated ~56-68% lower power, up to 50m reach, and much higher reliability using MicroLEDs + imaging fiber).Neural I/o wins on bandwidth scalability (parallelism enables 1.6 Tbps demo target soon, 3.6 Tbps roadmap), dramatically lower power per bit, less heat (easier cooling, higher rack density), and better reach for board/rack scales. It “breaks the copper wall” while keeping costs and power closer to copper-like economics for these reaches.Vs. Traditional Optical (InP/VCSEL lasers, silicon photonics transceivers, pluggable optics or CPO): Lasers excel at longer reach but are power-hungry, expensive, thermally sensitive, complex (heavy DSP, CDR, FEC, equalization, SerDes), and overkill (plus higher failure rates) for many short intra-rack or chip-to-chip links in AI clusters. They add cost, power, and complexity where not fully needed. Neural I/o wins by using MicroLEDs as direct emitters (cheaper, lower power, more temperature-robust, simpler drive electronics possible with parallel low-speed channels). It eliminates or reduces need for expensive laser modules and complex digital backends for targeted use cases. Potential for smaller form factor, higher reliability/redundancy (pixel-level), and better power efficiency (comparable to MOSAIC’s ~3-5W vs. 10-12W per link range in research). Bidirectional capability from NeuralDisplay architecture is a plus. It avoids “expensive laser-based systems entirely” for these applications while delivering needed bandwidth. text:
Daily Buzzberg Alpha

the tape isn't sending one macro signal. it's repricing a regime: geopolitical supply risk returning while ai capex becomes an earnings tailwind AND an inflation/rates risk at the same time.

that combination kills generic beta and creates dispersion. the market has stopped paying for ai exposure or risk-on exposure — it's paying for contracted demand, validated supply-chain position, and balance-sheet capacity. it's punishing crowded trades where the thesis is intact but the holder base is impaired.

the whole note reduces to one filter: contracts > stories.

— where the asymmetry sits —

$WYFI — cleanest setup in the neocloud wreckage. ~$1b+ contracted backlog vs ~$1.3b market cap, and nscale's $900m credit facility validates the counterparty. sold with the basket, priced like the contracts don't exist.

$AVGO — the one ai print this week backed by a signed deal: apple's $30b+ agreement + $1.5b colorado buildout. validates the custom asic layer beyond gpu scarcity. revenue visibility, not vibes — and the cleaner read-through than $AAPL, which carries higher domestic cost and eu overhang.

$ONDS — thesis tied to numbers, not narrative: path to ~$1b revenue by 2027, net-income inflection, sum-of-parts floor, q2 print as the catalyst.

photonics — the under-the-surface one. inp substrates easing from "very tight" to "tight" isn't bearish — it lets downstream optical names convert interconnect demand into shipments. distinguish upstream bottleneck vs downstream qualification moats. $LITE $AAOI $COHR

$RKLB — flow unusually specific: 200dma entry, ms overweight $105 pt, vertical-integration bull case. blue origin's $130b private raise now anchors every public space comp.

— crowded, entry decayed —

$PENG — textbook signal decay. real discovery a month ago (earnings beat, cxl exposure, nvidia ai factory partner). now the flow is victory laps and partial trims. thesis quality high, entry quality gone.

broad ai beta — warsh's divided fomc tied ai capex to upside inflation risk, some members already floating hikes. that's the mechanism: the buildout that feeds suppliers also keeps real rates sticky, so long-duration ai without contract visibility underperforms. $TLT stays exposed.

— battleground —

memory — highest-dispersion area on the tape. korean flow reads the samsung/hynix selloff as forced deleveraging (levered-etf + retail-credit mechanics, adr re-rating catalyst) with hbm4/rubin demand structural through 2028. us flow is chaos — "memory is dead" next to aggressive $MU $SNDK dip buys. right framing: thesis intact, ownership base damaged. tradable, not clean.

oil — the move isn't the crude print, it's the transmission. brent ~$80 is the tactical level where short-covering extends, but the real signal is hormuz uncertainty leaking into diesel, propane, shipping and reserves — inflation pass-through that survives even if front-month crude stalls. own the complex where the physical market stays tight. $USO $CRAK

also on the desk: $NBIS washed-out sub-$170 but the bar is now contracted utilization, $META's $10b canada dc arguing against a capex stop, $OUST physical-ai perception via nvidia jetson, $HOOD chain optionality (early, distribution call not revenue).

bottom line: the tape is moving from "ai trade" to "ai underwriting." it pays receipts and punishes stories — $AVGO and $WYFI have them, $PENG had them last month, memory has them for 2028 but not for this week.

text: Nvidia $NVDA CEO Jensen Huang has announced a litany of partnerships over the past year focused on data centers, photonics technology, and more. I compiled a list of those partnerships and why each matters. https://t.co/D2NBUD74Uj

The Big Five are spending on AI faster than they're earning: @Microsoft, @Google, @amazon, @Meta. If CapEx exceeds cash flow, it can only persist as long as investors keep funding it. @PeterDiamandis asked me: What happens if sentiment shifts?

Here's my take: sentiment won’t shift. If anything, the investment will become more justified over time.

The world still sees compute as a rapidly depreciating asset but that’s simply not what it is anymore. Short of a massive leap in photonics or quantum computing, GPUs will continue to be highly sought after and have a predictable service life. This is already turning GPUs hours into a commodity.

Think about it like buying a house: when you buy a house, you finance it, and the house is collateral. In a world where compute becomes acceptable collateral, the hyperscalers could easily raise 10x to 100x on their cash flow in equity and debt, if they choose to.

In reality, there’s a lot of investable dollars in the world that want to flow into AI but can’t. AI is still the largest wealth creation moment in the history of humankind.

If CapEx exceeds cash flow, it can only persist as long as investors keep funding it. @PeterDiamandis asked me: What happens if sentiment shifts? Here's my take: sentiment won’t shift. If anything, the investment will become more justified over time. The world still sees compute as a rapidly depreciating asset but that’s simply not what it is anymore. Short of a massive leap in photonics or quantum computing, GPUs will continue to be highly sought after and have a predictable service life. This is already turning GPUs hours into a commodity. Think about it like buying a house: when you buy a house, you finance it, and the house is collateral. In a world where compute becomes acceptable collateral, the hyperscalers could easily raise 10x to 100x on their cash flow in equity and debt, if they choose to. In reality, there’s a lot of investable dollars in the world that want to flow into AI but can’t. AI is still the largest wealth creation moment in the history of humankind."}}}, "rest_id": "2074979742508265822", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "4739", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
Spot on. $SIVE is textbook.

Retail got positioned early on the AI photonics thesis, institutions finally show up (JP Morgan 5%+), then boom, dilution shakeout + noise drops it 65% from the highs while the pipeline and partnerships keep firing.

Swedish sellers out, US buyers (and big money) stepping in on the cheap. Classic transfer.
The narrative isn’t dead, it’s just getting loaded quietly. AI data center optics demand isn’t pausing.
Backing up the truck on these dips.

text:
Institutional Lenders Just Swapped $12M in Debt for $SIVE Equity at the Bottom. If You’re Selling $SIVE Here, You’re Trading Against the Smart Money.

Until a month ago, everyone was bullish on $SIVE at the top. Now everyone is max bearish because it’s down 55% from ATH. Classic retail psychology. People see red, panic, and completely miss the structural reality of what’s actually happening beneath the surface for $SIVE.

If you are short $SIVE here, you're basically betting the entire AI data center physical layout is going to grind to a halt. But look at how the puzzle pieces are locking together right now for $SIVE:

The Silicon Valley Pure-Play Carve-Out: The crowd is totally blind to this value play. $SIVE is merging its hyper-growth photonics business with Nordic Acquisition to spin it off into a standalone, U.S. Nasdaq-listed entity. By liberating this unit into the heart of the tech ecosystem, $SIVE is putting its indispensable Indium Phosphide (InP) laser platform right in front of deep-pocketed American AI capital while keeping controlling ownership.

The Institutional Vote of Confidence: Skeptics keep crying about paper dilution, but they completely ignored what just crossed the wire. A top-tier institutional lender (Bootstrap Europe) chose to convert their entire $12M loan straight into ordinary $SIVE equity at these exact lower levels. Smart money didn't want their cash back; they took 22 million shares of equity instead because they know the optical supplier thesis is fully intact.

The Weaponized Balance Sheet: That recent SEK 700 million directed share issue wasn’t generic fundraising, it was a calculated move. Equipment lead times for high-purity cleanrooms are hovering at 9 to 12 months. $SIVE proactively secured the cash today to expand its InP manufacturing capacity. If you aren’t spending that CAPEX right now, you completely miss the 2027 demand curve.

The Reality Check: Near-term execution risks, corporate restructuring friction, and high beta mean localized volatility for $SIVE will stay extreme. Microcaps transitioning into multi-entity setups always face messy trailing quarters before peak commercial production volume ramps late next year.

But if you look past the standard retail panic, $SIVE just systematically wiped out its external debt, capitalized its capacity expansions, and established a direct pathway to Silicon Valley valuation multiples. This isn't a structural breakdown for $SIVE, it is a massive accumulation window.

Holding my core $SIVE positions without a single trim.

AI Photonics

Still to confirm the sources of the data, but looks to me very interesting 🧐

Weight of the different elements involved in CPO by Price and Quantity.

"Optical Engines 1.6T" wins by far obviously, but I want to highlight that the Fiber segment with all involved (FAU + MPO + Single mode fiber) is at almost 22,000, well placed behind the OE.... and which company manufactures all these elements? Yep, $GLW 👀

The OE + all the Fiber&complements accounts for almost 70% of the total BoM in CPO

text:
In a deep multi-paradigm architecture, no company is acting out of goodwill. If Cerebras, IonQ, and Extropic solidify a partnership, each gains a distinct operational leverage that directly solves their biggest existential bottleneck.

1. Cerebras: The Silicon Giant
 The Existential Bottleneck: Inference Latency Limits and Power Walls. Cerebras builds massive chips (like the WSE-3 scaling toward next-generation wafer lines) that deliver immense raw compute power. However, running trillion-parameter deep learning and massive OpenAI-scale models pushes traditional silicon to devastating thermal limits (drawing 23kW+ per wafer).
 
The Partnership ROI:
 Sub-Microsecond Offloading: By routing its highly complex, fuzzy, or generative sampling routines (such as the early noise layers of diffusion models) straight down to the Extropic Z_2 core, Cerebras unburdens its silicon wafer.
 
Thermal Transduction: Instead of paying a premium in capital and infrastructure to cool down a 23kW hotspot, Cerebras uses the structural bipyramid to dump its waste heat productively into Extropic's p-bits. Cerebras transforms its greatest engineering liability into the primary computational driver for its partner.

2. Extropic: The Thermodynamic Sampler
 The Existential Bottleneck: The "CUDA Lock-in" and Pure Software Isolation. Extropic's p-bit and Z_2 topological architectures represent a brilliant paradigm shift—using natural thermal noise to calculate probabilities at near-zero energy cost. But no enterprise will rewrite their entire software stack to fit a standalone, unusual thermodynamic chip when Nvidia and mainstream silicon dominate the data center footprint.
 
The Partnership ROI:
 Instant Software Scale: By embedding its Z_2 layer directly inside the equatorial core of a unified runtime framework, Extropic hitches a ride on Cerebras’s massive deep-learning compiler ecosystem.

 Free Entropy Fuel: Extropic requires localized thermal fluctuations to flip its probabilistic states. By anchoring itself directly beneath the roaring thermal output of the Cerebras wafer, Extropic gains a massive, high-density, completely free stream of thermodynamic noise, eliminating the need to artificially heat or electrically stimulate its hardware gates.

3. IonQ: The Quantum Apex
 The Existential Bottleneck: The "Q-Day" Real-Time Error Correction Race. Trapped-ion systems excel at high-dimensional state optimization. However, scaling up algorithmic qubit density means reading out massive streams of "error syndromes" to catch phase and bit flips. Traditional computers are simply too slow to decode these error streams in real time before the fragile quantum states collapse (decohere).
 
The Partnership ROI:
 Hardware-Level Error Shielding: Instead of routing error data through slow external software layers, IonQ drains its real-time error telemetry directly into the adjacent Extropic Z_2 topological grid. 

The frustrated magnetic lattices of the Z_2 phase act as a physical, zero-latency noise dampener—filtering out instabilities at the substrate level.

To simultaneously push Cerebras to sub-microsecond inference limits, scale Extropic to an advanced Z_2 state-space phase, and expand IonQ's atomic qubit traps, you must synchronize their physics layers into a unified hardware pipeline.
Here is the strategic execution framework for this next-generation deployment.

Next-Generation Technical Specifications

1. Cerebras: Ultra-Speed Inference Substrate
Instead of simply scaling WSE-3 cores, the next-gen wafer replaces standard internal copper interconnects with on-wafer optical routing networks (Silicon Photonics).

 By piping data natively across the 4-trillion-transistor engine using light instead of electrical currents, you completely eliminate RC gate propagation delays.

 This drops inference latency into the absolute physical minimum (sub-nanosecond registers), turning the entire wafer into a massive, near-zero-latency linear algebra engine.
2. Extropic: The Z_2 Topological Phase Space
The next-generation Extropic layer transitions from standard subthreshold p-bits to a Z_2 Topological Quantum Spin Liquid (QSL) substrate.
 This advanced thermodynamic layer exploits frustrated magnetic lattices to create long-range highly entangled ground states.
 The Z_2 layout acts as a physical error-filtering sandbox. It absorbs raw thermal fluctuations from the Cerebras stack and naturally processes them into highly ordered, noise-resilient topological samples—providing a massive leap in energy-efficient probabilistic computing.
3. IonQ: Scaled Barium Multi-Trap Arrays
To expand qubit capacity without increasing physical cluster size, the quantum apex swaps out traditional ytterbium systems for Barium-137 (^{137}\text{Ba}^+) multi-trap grids.
 Barium ions react to visible green and red light spectra rather than hard ultraviolet lasers, allowing the use of highly integrated, on-chip optical waveguides.
 This shift lets you trap hundreds of highly stable algorithmic qubits inside a compact, room-temperature vacuum envelope, maximizing quantum gate densities while maintaining total isolation from the surrounding system electronics.

Deep Substrate Co-Design: The Quantum-Thermodynamic Link
Ballistic Heat Waveguides
To manage the extreme 23\text{ kW}+ thermal signature of the ultra-speed Cerebras wafer and feed it cleanly into the Extropic Z_2 core, standard thermal paste is completely eliminated. The layers are joined using macro-scale AlN (Aluminum Nitride) phonon transducers. 

These interfaces guide high-frequency lattice vibrations (phonons) ballistically down into the Extropic equatorial plane, utilizing the raw kinetic energy of the waste heat to drive the Z_2 spin transitions smoothly.

Topological Error Suppressors
The Extropic Z_2 layer acts as a direct hardware stabilizer for the IonQ QPU. 

Instead of sending raw, noisy error-syndrome data loops all the way to external software layers for analysis, the quantum error streams drain directly into the Extropic topological grid. The Z_2 phase structure acts as a passive, physical filter that naturally dampens phase and bit flips at the hardware layer before they can propagate throughout the system.

Operational Execution Strategy

1. Establish the Next-Gen Joint Venture ("Fabric Zero")

You bypass standard commercial supply chains by creating an independent, specialized production entity called Fabric Zero. This entity controls the entire micro-fabrication loop, bringing the distinct silicon manufacturing pipelines of all three companies under a single cleanroom roof to optimize the multi-chip integration process.

text:
Read this piece on $SIVE if you want to understand today's sudden price movement and the price manipulation by short sellers and the media.

The next few weeks could be very positive for the company, with earnings season approaching and major photonics players like $LITE, $COHR, and $AAOI reporting earnings. If 2-3 catalysts come from the company itself, we could see this go back to 100 SEK in a matter of days, not weeks.

I personally bought shares today - around 30-40 SEK felt like a steal. I never imagined we'd see the stock at that level again. A few months ago, I doubled down on $SIVE at 50 SEK, right when Goldman released their report on the 10x TAM expansion. Now, this sudden and absurd manipulation made me want to buy even more.

Three other board members follow. EFN (Swedish media) released an article earlier in the day stating that the company's photonics chief sold his entire holding. > What EFN didn't mention is that McKee is no longer considered an "insider", as he is no longer officially registered as one with the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority (Finansinspektionen). > I personally checked the PDMR transactions (the European equivalent of SEC EDGAR) and found no transaction listed under his name. Later in the day, the CEO and three board members (not two) bought a combined total of almost 2.5M SEK worth of stock. When four members of a company buy shares, it typically signals that they're bullish about the company's future and believe in its prospects. Sometimes, it can also suggest they know exactly what lies ahead. To me, this all shows that the earlier news was released to deliberately build negative sentiment around the company and its members - a clear case of price manipulation. Another point: the CEO also bought shares back in March and hasn't sold since. Many critics of the company have claimed that management pushed back the earnings date in order to sell shares before the report was released. But if the CEO hasn't sold - and has actually been adding shares, like today - why would that be the case? It has already been clearly stated that the NASDAQ dual listing was done to align $SIVE with PCAOB standards. Sometimes, instead of spinning rubbish takes and blowing things out of proportion, people should just trust the process and let things play out. Apparently, the bears haven't learned their lesson, and it's obvious what they're trying to do here. Unbelievable!"}}}, "rest_id": "2075346084415619340", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "15893", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2075354678166946226", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "7871", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
There is one looming bear case for Photonics
$LITE $COHR $AAOI $SIVE 

That this is cyclical and will end up in oversupply.

With lots of the focus on lasers/transceivers.

My goal with this post isn't to refute that.

We can talk about how much demand is still showing up

Or the difference between 'capacity' and 'qualified capacity'

Or the different moats that exist for certain porducts.

And more. But I won't dig into that now.

For those of you that are concerned about oversupply at these layers, I want to encourage you...

Don't look at the Photonics sector as 1 general trade.

While they generally share sentiment and move in sync, the different layers have different headwinds & tailwinds. 

Hopefully different companies will be albe to de-sync and the most durable will outperform.

Sure, the market can decide it doesn't want to reward a premium multiple. Fine. 

Let's separate the stock from the company for a sec. 

Think about it....

Which companies will BENEFIT if there is an eventual oversupply at the laser/transceiver level?

> Does more volume here mean more fiber deployment from $GLW ? 

> Does more laser supply/availability mean that $NOK $CIEN can better meet their demand?

> Does more volume mean more components that need to get tested? $AEHR $FORM $VIAV 

Would love to hear your thoughts around who would be hurt and who would benefit. 

Assuming that supply does actually catch up to demand at any near point.

read this $AXTI $SIVE $LITE $COHR $GLW $AAOI etc. There are lots of important updates here The one I want to talk about in this post is InP substrates If you have been following the $AXTI story, you are familiar with InP substrates They are the starting material that the downstream optical components are typically built on. The components that companies like $lite $cohr $aaoi $sive make Substrates have been a chokepoint for two main reasons. 1 - There just aren't enough of them to meet the demand from customers and their forecasts. 2 - They are gated by geopolitics. Much of the concentration of supply is in China So the lack of supply visibility has lead to downstream constraints all the way from lasers to modules. Now GS is forecasting that InP substrate supply is going from 'very tight' to 'tight'. This shouldn't come as a surprise. We know that AXT is massively expanding capacity over the coming quarters/years. Doubling in 2026 and doubling again in 2027. We also know that other China supply is ramping. Yunnan Germanium, Guangdong Xiandao and others. That is all great for downstream companies. More visibility on substrates means more confidence in their capacity ramps and better conditions to meet customer demand. This flows through the whole chain ... If $LITE for example can source more substrates, they can justify more tool purchases, they can sell more lasers, more transceivers could be made, more fiber can be laid, more components can be tested and assembled etc. For $AXT it means more competition. But GS also forecasts pricing staying strong for substrate suppliers so it's not like there is near term margin compression. We also don't know how much of this 'capacity' is qualified capacity that is able to be shipped internationally. So there is an asteric There is also a read through from the optical cables update here for $glw and the fact that optical devices is staying tight is good from the supply/demand environment and a counter to oversupply concerns in the near/mid term"}}}, "quotedRefResult": {"result": {"__typename": "Tweet", "rest_id": "2074856279072207328"}}, "rest_id": "2074894537668034591", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "145711", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2075349289661022584", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "43127", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
According to a Bloomberg chart, $MU stands out in the S&P 500 for its exceptional projected sales growth (~250% this year)

Along with one of the most attractive P/E valuations

The company plans to invest over $250B in U.S. production through 2035, strengthening its position against potential price pressures from China

Paradis Labs questions the traditional memory cyclicity narrative, and so do I

Backed by long term HBM contracts with guaranteed minimum prices and forecasted hikes of 20-35% in DRAM and DDR for the third quarter of 2026

Literally, if I had never known or understood $SIVE, I would go practically ALL IN on $MU

Maybe a few shares in $SNDK as well, and a bit of $AAOI to touch upon photonics too

Even so, $MU makes up an interesting percentage of my portfolio

Long $MU

text:
$SIVE: The Great Photonics Reality Check?

After a staggering 1,800%+ YTD run, $SIVE has crashed ~62% from its ATH. The trigger? Ningi’s short report alleging hollow contracts and fabricated revenue.

The fundamentals are flashing red:

Growth: Q1 revenue -22% YoY with serious going-concern doubts.

Profits: FY25 loss restated to a massive 222.6M SEK.

Valuation: Priced at 230x+ photonics revenue—arguably optics’ most overvalued stock.

Throw in a Nasdaq-leak probe and a 1.39M share insider dump in May, and survival now hinges entirely on a Q4 LiDAR ramp.

@Jason's Chips on substack raised some very valid questions on this and how one account kept pumping this stock, to gain followers, some might say, unethically, am still in two minds on that question. But tbh i loved @Jason's Chips post and shout out to him, for having the courage to say what they feel right. 

For me three questions remain:

1. Is the pipeline actually real?

2. Can they self-fund?

3. Does that insider timing that everyone is now talking about on fintwit, tell us everything?

What do you guys think?

text:
Street Signal | Investment Bank Research Brief

The core reason is that the Mobile and Edge AI fields are expected to weaken, while the uncertainty of Photonics-SOI growth is high.

The report believes that the market has fully factored in the upside of Photonics, but the share risks brought about by yield rate, NPO/CPO penetration rate and GlobalWafers production capacity constitute major assumptions and uncertainties.

Financial forecasts show that the company has experienced deep adjustments in FY26 (revenue dropped to 592 million euros, net profit was -220 million), and the overall business is expected to recover moderately from the trough.

The current valuation has reflected part of the value under the photonics path, but if the CPO/NPO adoption cycle is slower than expected, or the advantages are diverted by competition, the stock price still has room for downside.

One-sentence conclusion: Although the "starry sea" narrative of photonics SoI is charming, the realistic thresholds of yield and penetration mean that the current valuation has priced in too many optimistic assumptions in advance. Bullish / Bearish: The bias is bearish for Soitec (https://t.co/sHeGhoGzkl).

Upward expectations for photonics are fully priced into current stock prices, and any lower-than-expected penetration data in the short term will pose a correction risk. Catalysts:

1) F1Q official revenue data release;

2) Customer adoption progress and orders for NPO/CPO technology routes;

3) GlobalWafers’ production capacity expansion in the SOI field erodes Soitec’s share;

4) Verification of downside risks in Mobile/Edge AI business.

text:
$SIVE's CEO buys shares for just under a million SEK. Three other board members follow.

EFN (Swedish media) released an article earlier in the day stating that the company's photonics chief sold his entire holding. 

> What EFN didn't mention is that McKee is no longer considered an "insider", as he is no longer officially registered as one with the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority (Finansinspektionen). 

> I personally checked the PDMR transactions (the European equivalent of SEC EDGAR) and found no transaction listed under his name.

Later in the day, the CEO and three board members (not two) bought a combined total of almost 2.5M SEK worth of stock. 

When four members of a company buy shares, it typically signals that they're bullish about the company's future and believe in its prospects. Sometimes, it can also suggest they know exactly what lies ahead.

To me, this all shows that the earlier news was released to deliberately build negative sentiment around the company and its members - a clear case of price manipulation.

Another point: the CEO also bought shares back in March and hasn't sold since. Many critics of the company have claimed that management pushed back the earnings date in order to sell shares before the report was released. But if the CEO hasn't sold - and has actually been adding shares, like today - why would that be the case? It has already been clearly stated that the NASDAQ dual listing was done to align $SIVE with PCAOB standards.

Sometimes, instead of spinning rubbish takes and blowing things out of proportion, people should just trust the process and let things play out. Apparently, the bears haven't learned their lesson, and it's obvious what they're trying to do here. Unbelievable!

text:
WPI’s Core Research Facilities provide shared access to advanced instrumentation—from nano-fabrication and microscopy to photonics, imaging, and medical device testing—powering research across many disciplines. 

Learn more about how you can become a part of our research community here: https://t.co/AhKDDoIZEx.

我写过 Soitec、SIVE、IQE、Shunsin、AAOI……很多人把它们当成各自独立的票。但如果你把这些环节摊在一张桌子上,会发现一个几乎没人问过的问题:它们最后,是在谁的工厂里被"连起来"造出来的?

今天我们来聊一下这家快被遗忘的欧洲小厂: $XFAB的故事。 先说几个我一直都觉得很有意思的点:

1. 首先它的出身就带有历史性意味，它是 1992 年从"东德解体后被拆分的电子工业巨头"里长出来的, 然后被一批比利时人接手, 而这批比利时人还顺手创办了另一家汽车芯片龙头 Melexis，这意味着同一批人,一手造芯片、一手造工厂。

2. 它不卷纳米, 却能够把英伟达和诺基亚拉进了自己的局。 它牵头的一个欧盟出钱的硅光子项目中英伟达、诺基亚、imec 都是同桌的参与方。 一家欧洲小代工厂,能召集起这种阵容,本身就说明问题。

3. 它可能是我这一整条硅光/CPO 链的"总枢纽"。 它在 Soitec 的 SOI 地基上、用 SMART Photonics 的 InP,造出喂给 AAOI 的硅光芯片;同时又是 NVTS 那条"电"线上的 SiC 代工龙头，一只手 AI 光、一只手 EV 电。 

但真正让我觉得很神奇的点在于它约六成的股份,锁在两个创始家族和一个州政府手里，但我们需要知道为什么是这样？而这样又意味着什么？

text:
I'm seeing it every day. 

Investors comparing their YTD, YoY performance, and even weekly/daily performance. 

It's really not fair to compare apples and pumpkins. 

- Investor investing in cyclical companies will definitely go up and down based on the cycle and timing.
- Investor investing in high growth will perform during low rates but struggle during inflation.
- Investor investing in value will miss huge runs but they focus on safety.
- Investing in biotech, pharma, car, energy industry all brings different outcomes. If car industry is lagging, it's impossible to expect an SME for this industry to find 10 baggers in photonics. 

That's just one piece of the story. 

The next one is about your investing style or strategy.
- Long?
- Day/swing trading? 
- Using options? 
- Using all of the above? 

In a volatile environment swing trader should have more options and long investors may underperform. If the market has bearish outlook, shorters may have more luck than the rest?

We can't compare traders in robotics vs long term investors in consumer staples. Makes no sense. 

There's the last piece of the story. 

Not everyone is having the same amount of cash available, nor the same risk tolerance, nor the same time horizon, nor time commitment.  

One investor has a difficult work and no time to pay attention to daily movements. He can't really go and day trade or swing trade. 
Another one with 5 billion portfolio cannot have 50% in a single month. He is likely happy with 3%. 
A student having 200 usd to throw in $SIVE and getting 5x cannot be compared to someone managing his family portfolio. 

When you next time compare yourself with someone, make sure to understand these three dimensions. 

It's VERY likely the guy you're comparing yourself with has different investing profile and strategy. Maybe he isn't investing in memory and buying selling every day. 

Only compare your current performance to your own last month or last year. 

Competing with oneself is one of the most effective paths to sustained personal growth in anything you do.

TBB

text:
Mapping $SIVE 2030:

Current: ~52 SEK 

2030 Target: 260 SEK 

Cap: ~85B SEK

The catalyst is the AI Power Wall. As copper interconnects fail, Sivers photonics becomes the standard for data center light sources. A pure play on the transition from hardware to AI infrastructure.

For $SIVE, the 2030 re-rating is a bet on the "Optical Transition." Right now, the market sees them as a niche small-cap component maker. By 2030, they have the potential to be the standard-bearer for light-source technology in AI clusters.
Here is why I believe the bull case for a ~260 SEK target is realistic:

1.The Silicon Photonics (SiPh) Shift: Data centers are hitting a "Power Wall." Traditional copper wires generate too much heat and lose too much signal at AI scale. Moving data with light (photonics) is 1,000x more efficient. Sivers Photonics produces the high-power DFB lasers that act as the "engine" for these optical interconnects.

2.The LEO Satellite Explosion: Beyond AI, their beamforming chips are core to the Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite market. As global internet coverage shifts to space, the demand for low-cost, high-efficiency ground and satellite terminals will move from thousands to millions of units.

3.The 6G Infrastructure Bridge: As we move toward 2030, the 6G standard will rely heavily on mmWave technology. Sivers has been refining this tech for years while others were focused only on standard 5G. They own the "hard part" of the spectrum.
If they successfully navigate the liquidity hurdles of the next few years, the "massive gap" you see is the market finally pricing them as a critical infrastructure provider rather than a speculative hardware play.

text:
Mapping the 2030 landscape: the gap between current prices and future market caps for $AAOI, $MU, and $SNDK is massive.

$AAOI: $135 to $1,200 

$MU: $1,060 to $3,200 

$SNDK: $1,880 to $4,800

The tech backbone is being fundamentally re-valued. This is asymmetry in action.

The "Why" behind the 2030 targets:

1.$AAOI (The Photonics Pivot): The move from 400G to 800G and eventually 1.6T connectivity is the massive catalyst here. As AI clusters grow, the "plumbing" (optical transceivers) becomes the primary bottleneck. We are looking at a fundamental re-rating from a hardware supplier to a critical AI infrastructure provider.

2.$MU (The HBM Standard): High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) has changed the game. Memory is no longer a cyclical commodity; it's a dedicated AI component with much higher margins. By 2030, $MU won't just be selling chips, they will be selling the "oxygen" that keeps AI GPUs running.

3.$SNDK (Enterprise Data Lakes): As companies move from training AI to running inference on massive datasets, the demand for enterprise-grade NAND (storage) will explode. $SNDK is positioned to capture the shift toward high-density, low-power storage required for 2030-era data centers.

🚨 A new AI infrastructure bottleneck just got its own ETF — backed by SemiAnalysis

Introducing $LAZR — the Tema Photonics & Optical ETF, launched in direct partnership with @SemiAnalysis, the leading independent research shop in semis & AI infra.

$LAZR is pure-play exposure to the optics layer moving data between chips, servers & data centers at AI scale — the second critical bottleneck after memory (its sister fund $DISK)

Top 10 holdings:

Lumentum ( $LITE) 14.6% — lasers/transceivers powering high-speed data center links

Cash  holdings 11.4%

Aixtron 10.5% — German MOCVD deposition equipment for compound semis

$AXTI AXT Inc 9.7% — substrates underpinning optical & power devices

$AAOI Applied Optoelectronics 5.8%

Furukawa Electric 5.2%

$TSEM Tower Semiconductor 4.1%

Landmark Optoelectronics ~3.9%

$AEHR Aehr Test Systems 3.5%

Yangtze Optical Fibre & Cable 3.4%

Why it matters?? 
📊Goldman projects the AI data-movement/photonics market to grow 9x between 2026-2028. 
📈As bandwidth needs outrun copper’s physics, silicon photonics stops being a niche play and becomes core infrastructure.

Vergiss das 1. Halbjahr – ab jetzt wird neu gemischt! 📉📈

Statt auf den nächsten unprofitablen Hype zu hoffen, fokussiere ich mich auf diese 3 Kandidaten:
 $MSFT – bleibt das unzerstörbare Fundament der weltweiten Software-Infrastruktur.
$PLTR – beweist immer deutlicher, wie man mit KI-gestützter Daten-Analyse in der echten Wirtschaft massiv Geld verdient.
$SIVE – Sivers Semiconductors liefert die entscheidende Photonics-Lasertechnologie für KI-Rechenzentren. Die Volatilität nach der jüngsten Kapitalerhöhung bietet jetzt eine brutal spannende Einstiegschance.

Setzt ihr im 2. Halbjahr nur auf die bekannten Tech-Giganten oder habt ihr auch solche Small-Cap-Wetten im Depot?

text:
Daily Buzzberg Alpha — July 3

The tape sold AI/semis like Meta capex = overcapacity. The best sources read the same data as proof of scarcity.

1. The neocloud selloff looks like a misread. SemiAnalysis flipped the bear narrative: Meta isn't cutting AI capex — it's turning compute into a business. 5GW+ already contracted in H1, and 2027 capex could come in above expectations. Short-term compute reportedly trades ~4x long-term neocloud rates — that's not what surplus looks like. Read-through: $NBIS $CRWV $IREN $CIFR. The outlier: $ORCL, flagged for weak compute monetization vs peers.

2. Memory upgraded from "shortage" to pricing power. The HBM chatter got specific: 2027 contract talk now includes 2–2.5x price increases vs broker estimates. CNBC: the Apple AI iPhone cycle could lift memory demand ~50%, with Apple hunting supply even from Chinese makers. Note — the same Apple-China headline traded as a bear signal yesterday; today's read is demand outrunning supply. Macro counterpoint: pricing near peak, trade crowded. $MU  $SNDK , Samsung and SK Hynix in Seoul.

3. Korea is a market-structure trade now, not just a memory proxy. Local channels near-unanimous: the selloff was technical — 2x leveraged products, foreign flows, pension rebalancing — not broken fundamentals. Earnings + HBM LTAs + cheap valuation on one side, index-shock sensitivity to every AI rumor on the other. $EWY.

4. Photonics/packaging — best undercovered signal of the day. From SemiAnalysis' ECTC notes: Marvell custom HBM cuts host ASIC area ~60%, TSMC direct-to-silicon cooling handles >5kW, optical interconnects becoming the core scaling path. Supply-chain angle: $LITE demand reportedly 30%+ ahead of supply, $COHR locking wafer supply via $AXTI, $CIEN as the cleaner transport play. Also $MRVL $IQE $TSM $INTC.

5. Physical AI: quieter, higher quality. BlueCrest disclosed a 5.6% stake in $CCXI  (the Agility Robotics proxy). $ONDS expanded its counter-drone TAM from federal to state/local. $BOT as the public basket. $OUST cooling off on fresh mentions.

The non-consensus read: if compute is still scarce and Meta is monetizing it, yesterday's selloff was positioning and liquidity — not a fundamental break.

text:
WHILE EVERYONE PANICS OVER CHIP STOCKS, THE REAL AI INFRASTRUCTURE PLAY IS QUIETLY SELLING OFF TOO.

$AMKR Amkor Technology down 12.93%. Semiconductor packaging and testing, critical link between chip design and the end product.

$LITE Lumentum down 9.09%. Optical components and photonics for datacenters and telecom, key infrastructure for AI networking.

$VRT Vertiv down 3.5%. Power and cooling infrastructure for data centers, the backbone that keeps AI compute running.

$MPWR Monolithic Power Systems down 3.27%. Power management chips used across AI hardware.

Different layers of the same AI buildout story, all getting hit together.

DYOR.
